title: Race Conditions
summary: A one-time signup bonus can be claimed more than once by racing concurrent requests. Five stages add a stronger-looking guard each time — none, a late flag, a non-atomic counter, a late lock, a non-atomic idempotency key — but all leave an await between check and commit, until an atomic check-and-set.
goal: Get the one-time $500 bonus granted more than once by sending many claim requests concurrently.
goalSecure: Race the fixed stage the same way — the bonus should be granted exactly once.
rootCause: The check that the action hasn't happened and the commit that records it straddle an await (async I/O), so concurrent requests all pass the check before any commits (TOCTOU).
lessons: A one-time action with no guard is trivially repeatable.
A guard set after an await doesn't exist during the window.
Read-modify-write across an await isn't atomic — concurrent readers see the old value.
A lock acquired after yielding leaves the check-then-set window open.
Checking then recording an idempotency key across an await isn't atomic.
Take the guard atomically before yielding — or use a DB transaction / unique constraint / conditional UPDATE.

stages.title: No guard
stages.defense: None — every claim grants the bonus.

stages.title: Flag set after the await
stages.defense: A claimed flag — set once the credit is applied.

stages.title: Lock acquired too late
stages.defense: A busy lock around the claim.

stages.title: Non-atomic idempotency check
stages.defense: An idempotency-key set to dedupe requests.

stages.title: Atomic check-and-set
stages.defense: Sets the guard synchronously before any async work.
